assertions.text: Output contains a one-sentence logline naming the subject, conflict or journey, and stakes
Episode structure section explains what changes across the series, not just topic lists
Broadcaster rationale references the named platform's actual programming priorities
Pitch opens with a hook, not the title or a logline
Main pitch body is 150-180 words (approximately 60 seconds at natural speaking pace)
Pitch ends with a sentence that invites a 'tell me more' response
Email body is under 250 words including sign-off
Project proposition is stated in two sentences or fewer
The ask is specific: one clear next step, not an open-ended invitation
Opens with a compelling hook, not 'This project is a...'
Craft and methodology are described, not just outcomes
Closing paragraph adds meaning rather than repeating the opening
Opening paragraph frames the strategic opportunity, not the logistics
Mutual benefit is specific and concrete for both parties
Closes with one specific, actionable next step
